preferences.label: Stock
preferences.description: Uses the stock rocket launcher.

preferences.label: Original
preferences.description: Uses the original rocket launcher.

preferences.label: Mangler
preferences.description: Uses the cow mangler rocket launcher.

preferences.label: Bounce
preferences.description: Crouched bounce setup, meaning you can use a rocket to "bounce" the moment you land. Requires teleheight < 2.0 units.

preferences.label: Bhop
preferences.description: Bhop (bounce hop) setup, meaning you can jump the moment you land. It is also possible to do standing bounce with same setup. Requires teleheight < 2.0 units.

preferences.label: Jumpbug
preferences.description: Jumpbug setup, meaning you can jump the moment you land if you release crouch and jump at the same exact time. Requires teleheight < TODO.

preferences.label: Simple
preferences.description: Setups that are simple. Requiring few/no binds, few inputs, and few rockets.

preferences.label: Consistency
preferences.description: Setups avoiding diagonal movement, which can be inconsistent without a bind. Also prefer bounce setups where the timing window for hitting the bounce is large.

preferences.label: No bind required
preferences.description: Setups that use no binds.

preferences.label: Auto bounce
preferences.description: Can be bounced by only holding m1. Note that some setups require aiming straight down (fully automatic), and others require aiming forward (half automatic).

preferences.label: Auto synced bounce
preferences.description: It is possible to prefire a rocket for a synced bounce only holding m1. Note that some setups require aiming straight down (fully automatic), and others require aiming forward (half automatic).

preferences.label: Auto standing bounce
preferences.description: Can be standing bounced by only holding m1. Perfect setup if you want to go straight into a pogo. Note that some setups require aiming straight down (fully automatic), and others require aiming forward (half automatic).

preferences.label: Auto synced standing bounce
preferences.description: It is possible to prefire a rocket for a synced standing bounce only holding m1. Note that some setups require aiming straight down (fully automatic), and others require aiming forward (half automatic).

preferences.label: Height
preferences.description: The heighest point your feet reach during the setup.

preferences.label: Distance
preferences.description: The horizontal distance traversed from leaving the initial platform to hitting the bounce/bhop/powerbounce.

preferences.label: Speed
preferences.description: The horizontal speed when leaving the initial platoform. This is often maximized by multi-rocket setups that hit a speedshot.

preferences.label: Compact
preferences.description: The horizontal distance traversed on the initial platform. This can be used to avoid setups that require large amount of space.

preferences.label: Quick
preferences.description: The time taken from the start of the setup to hitting the bounce/bhop/jumpbug.

preferences.label: Standing bounce
preferences.description: Setups where you have time to fire a rocket in order to do a standing bounce.

preferences.label: Power bounce
preferences.description: Setups where you can both bhop and have time to shoot a rocket in order to do a "power bounce".

preferences.label: Jumpbug power bounce
preferences.description: Setups where you can both jumpbug and have time to shoot a rocket in order to do a "jumpbug power bounce".

preferences.label: Synced bounce
preferences.description: Setups where you have time to prefire a rocket in order to perform a synced bounce.

preferences.label: Synced powerbounce
preferences.description: Setups where you have time to prefire a rocket in order to perform a synced power bounce,

preferences.label: Synced jumpbug powerbounce
preferences.description: Setups where you have time to prefire a rocket in order to perform a synced jumpbug power bounce.

preferences.label: Crouched start
preferences.description: Setups starting with being crouched. May not be preferable because of low horizontal speed.

preferences.label: Non-moving starts
preferences.description: Setups where you do not press any movement keys. May be preferable because of consistency.

preferences.label: Diagonal movement
preferences.description: Setups with diagonal movement, which, unless a bind is used, may be inconsistent.

preferences.label: +moveup
preferences.description: Setups involving the +moveup command. This allows the player to accelerate/walk slower.

preferences.label: +strafe
preferences.description: Setups involving +strafe together with +left or +right. This allows the player to walk in directions/speeds that are otherwise impossible.

preferences.label: No bind required
preferences.description: Setups that can be performed using just WASD. Note that a bind might still be preferable for consistency in the case of diagonal movement.

preferences.label: Quickswap
preferences.description: Setups that start by quickswapping to delay the first rocket. The player needs to be holding a different weapon than their rocket launcher for this to work.

preferences.label: Rocketless setup
preferences.description: Setups that involve no rockets in the initial action. Note that setups may still require rockets to hit for example a bounce.

preferences.label: 1 Rocket setup
preferences.description: Setups that use at most 1 rocket. (Excluding any rocket used to hit a bounce).

preferences.label: Jump shoot
preferences.description: Setups that start with a "Jump shot", shooting and jumping at the same time.

preferences.label: Jump duck shoot
preferences.description: Setups that start with a "Jump duck shot", shooting and crouched jumping at the same time.

preferences.label: Ctap jump duck shoot
preferences.description: Setups that start with a "CTAP jump duck shot", shooting and ctap jumping at the same time. This is preferable to maximize height.

preferences.label: Shoot 1 tick early
preferences.description: CTAP JDS setups that require manually shooting a rocket 1 tick before performing the CTAP, resulting in more height and speed.

preferences.label: Shoot 2 ticks early
preferences.description: CTAP JDS setups that require manually shooting a rocket 2 ticks before performing the CTAP, resulting in the most powerful CTAP possible. Useful for jumps like jump_diabarha last.

preferences.label: No bind required
preferences.description: Setups that only involve jumping or firing a rocket.
